text:
Once upon a time there lived a little squirrel named Sammy.

Sammy loved collecting nuts. They were his favorite thing. Not carrots. Not apples. Just nuts.

One autumn afternoon Sammy was greedily stuffing squirrels' favors into his sack.

Then something strange happened. An autumn storm hit. Rain filled his bag. Stars twinkled through the darkness.

By dusk an owl sneaked into Sammy's bush. "You've got plenty," it scolded. An unusual owl. Maybe that's why the owl seemed upset.

"But those are my favourite stars," Sammy whimpered. They weren't just ordinary nuts.

The owl sighed. "You've lost them."

Sammy didn’t know what stars looked like. He wished he could see them again.

That night stars filled his mind. They weren't even real. They were just images. It didn’t matter.

Next morning the stars were gone. Sammy was heartbroken.

That year the squirrels sang.

Sammy listened. So did
